source_jobs.title: Head of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

We're a small team that believes providing access to better financial insights and tools improves people’s lives, so we’re taking money matters into our own hands.



We’re building a new kind of financial platform that empowers people to take control of their finances, make informed decisions, and achieve their goals. We're a high-energy, fast-paced startup looking for determined, hands-on folks to join our team and make a real impact on people's financial lives.



The Position

Copilot is looking for a Head of Engineering to partner closely with our CTO and lead the day-to-day management of our entire Engineering team. This includes our talented iOS, Web, and Backend engineers across our New York City 🇺🇸 and Santiago, Chile 🇨🇱 offices.



As we continue scaling our app across platforms, we’re looking for a strong engineering leader who can ensure our engineering organization continues to deliver a best-in-class product while evolving our development practices to embrace AI-assisted engineering. You will help us maintain the high velocity and collaborative culture that have defined our success to date.



This is a hybrid position based in New York City. You’ll be required to come into our office at least 2 times a week along with the rest of the team. We work from home the rest of the week, but the office is always available in case you want to make use of it more often.



Your Responsibilities

 - Support and develop engineering managers and team leads across iOS, Web, and Backend. Set clear goals, ensure strong execution, and build a high-performing, collaborative team.

 - Drive the adoption of AI-native engineering workflows (like Claude Code, GitHub Copilot or Cursor) to amplify the team's output while maintaining strict standards for code quality and architectural integrity.

 - Help scale our engineering processes, including cycle planning, prioritization, hiring, and internal communication.

 - Partner with Product, Design, and Operations teams to align on company goals and ensure efficient, transparent roadmapping and delivery.

 - Work closely with our CTO and Staff Engineers to shape and refine our technical strategy, architecture, and platform investments.

 - Champion a strong engineering culture rooted in trust, inclusion, and autonomy, where AI is used to automate repetitive tasks, allowing our engineers to focus on complex problem solving and technical excellence.



Required Qualifications

 - 10+ years of experience in Software Engineering, with around 4 years in a leadership or management role.

 - Proven experience managing cross-functional and multi-location teams.

 - Background in consumer product development, preferably in high-scale mobile or fintech apps.

 - Deep understanding of software development best practices across frontend and backend, with a clear perspective on how to maintain engineering rigor and expert oversight in an AI-accelerated environment.

 - Passion for building simple, elegant solutions to complex technical and human challenges.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

 - Based in New York City or able to commute to our NYC office at least twice a week.



Desired Qualifications

 - Experience in a startup or high-growth environment, ideally at the Series A/B/C stage.

 - Exposure to or familiarity with our tech stack: Node.js, TypeScript, Firebase, Swift, React.

 - Experience leading teams in an AI-assisted environment, with a focus on leveraging LLMs to accelerate development cycles without sacrificing system reliability or security.

 - Experience scaling engineering processes and teams.

 - Previous involvement in recruiting, hiring, and team design at a strategic level.

 - Understanding of DevOps, CI/CD, observability, and data-driven product iteration.



Compensation and Benefits

 - Annual Salary of $230k to $260k

 - Equity in the company

 - Health, Dental & Vision Insurance (company pays 100% premium for employee)

 - 401k matching up

source_jobs.title: Senior Fullstack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Copilot Money is on a mission to help people stress less about money. We’re building a new kind of financial platform that empowers people to take control of their finances, make informed decisions, and achieve their goals. We're a high-energy, fast-paced startup looking for determined, hands-on folks to join our team and make a real impact on people's financial lives.



The Role

As a Senior Fullstack Engineer at Copilot Money, you'll be a key contributor on a small, high-ownership engineering team building one of the most trusted personal finance apps in the market. You'll work across the full stack on features that matter - bringing strong technical judgment, a bias for shipping, and a genuine enthusiasm for using AI tools as part of how you build.



This is a hybrid position based in New York City you’ll be required to come into our office at least 2 times a week along with the rest of the team. We work from home the rest of the week, but the office is always available in case you want to make use of it more often.



Key Responsibilities

 - Own end-to-end delivery of product features spanning web client and backend systems, from technical design through launch and iteration.

 - Design and implement backend APIs, data models, and services that power Copilot Money's core financial features.

 - Partner closely with Product and Design in a build-as-you-plan process - comfortable moving forward with incomplete inputs and refining solutions through real usage.

 - Contribute to architecture decisions and raise the bar for code quality, testing, and operational reliability within your team.

 - Actively use AI tools and models as part of your daily development workflow.

 - Identify and address technical debt and risks proactively, with a pragmatic approach to balancing speed and maintainability.



Required Qualifications

 - 6+ years of professional software engineering experience with significant ownership of shipped products.

 - Strong experience building web apps, with production work in Node.js, React, and TypeScript.

 - Strong backend engineering skills, including designing APIs, data models, and scalable services.

 - Experience with a major cloud provider (GCP, Cloudflare, or AWS).

 - A bonus if you have experience with: agentic interfaces/tools, Progress Web Apps, Firebase, real-time systems, Mobile apps, CI / CD systems

 - Comfortable working across the full stack with minimal hand-offs between client and server work.

 - Hands-on, day-to-day user of AI coding tools (e.g. Claude, Cursor, Gemini) - not just curious about AI, but actively integrating it into how you build.

 - Strong system design fundamentals and comfort making technical tradeoffs under ambiguity.

 - Clear communicator who works well with product, design, and fellow engineers.

 - Team player who can manage time independently.

 - Based in NYC.



Why should you work with us?

 - A product people love: Copilot users are extremely engaged with the app and are sharing it with their friends and family.

 - A team that cares: We’re a bunch of geeks who care about data quality and good coffee. We go the extra mile when it comes to making our users happy (and making an excellent pour-over).

 - Significant impact: We are currently a small, thirty-five-person team with big ambitions. Our team has previous experience at companies like Google and Apple.

 - Multicultural team: We’re based in NYC 🇺🇸 and in Santiago, Chile 🇨🇱.

 - Great company culture: You’ll work with a highly collaborative team that’s always learning and ready to help each other out.



Compensation and Benefits

 - The expected salary range for this role is $185k - $215k. However, the starting base pay will depend on a number of factors including the candidate’s location, skills, experience, market demands, and internal pay parity.

 - Equity in the company

 - Health, Dental & Vision Insurance (company pays 100% premium for employee)

 - 401k matching up to 4%

 - Home

source_jobs.title: Senior Web Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

We're a small team that believes providing access to better financial insights and tools improves people’s lives, so we’re taking money matters into our own hands.



Our product https://copilot.money/ is a personal finance management app for iOS and Mac that people actually love using https://twitter.com/copilotmoney. Copilot takes advantage of machine learning to deliver powerful, hyper-personalized finance data insights to help users effortlessly understand and manage their spending, budgeting, and investing. We also use crowdsourced feedback to continuously improve the experience.



Apple recently named us as a finalist for the 2024 Apple Design Awards https://developer.apple.com/design/awards/, and has named Copilot one of the best finance apps in the App Store. And we're only getting started!



Why should you work with us?

A product people love: Copilot users are extremely engaged with the app and are sharing it with their friends and family.



A team that cares: We’re a bunch of geeks who care about data quality and good coffee. We go the extra mile when it comes to making our users happy (and making an excellent pour-over).



Significant impact: We are currently a small, twenty five-person team with big ambitions. Our team has previous experience at companies like Google and Apple.



Multicultural team: We’re based in NYC 🇺🇸 and in Santiago, Chile 🇨🇱



Great company culture: You’ll work with a highly collaborative team that’s always learning and ready to help each other out.



The Position

We’ve been able to craft a best-in-class app for iOS and Mac, and now want to bring Copilot to the web with the same level of user experience. To achieve this, we’re looking for a Senior Web Engineer to join us in the development of our new platform, and contribute to the building of an all-star Web team, of which you’d be the third member.



This is a hybrid position based in Santiago, Chile. You’ll be required to come into our office at least 2 times a week along with the rest of the team. We work from home the rest of the week, but the office is always available in case you want to make use of it more often.



Your Responsibilities

 - Collaborate closely with the Product and Design teams to define and implement new features and constantly improve the ones we already have.

 - Maintain and improve the quality and performance of the web app.

 - Implement unit and UI tests for new features to maintain and improve reliability.



Required Qualifications

 - 5+ years of experience working on Web apps on the frontend side

 - 3+ years of experience with TypeScript and React

 - Knowledge of React frameworks and built tools

 - Strong understanding of responsive design and cross-browser compatibility

 - Experience with asynchronous request handling

 - An eye for good design and attention to detail

 - Team player who can manage time independently

 - English proficiency; all of our meetings and internal communications are in English

 - Based in Santiago, Chile



Desired Qualifications

 - Bachelor in Computer Science or equivalent

 - Knowledge of performance and scalability techniques

 - Knowledge of web accessibility standards and best practices

 - Experience with: Progress Web Apps, Google Cloud / Firebase / Firestore, Mobile apps, CI / CD systems

Compensation and Benefits

 - Monthly salary of CLP $3.000.000 - $3.600.000 (líquido).

 - Equity in the company (Stock options)

 - Home Office Setup

 - Complementary health insurance (in process)

 - Remote work allowance (CLP $50.000/month)Wellness allowance (CLP $85.000/month)

 - We cover sick days that your health insurance doesn’t pay (usually the first 3 days of a medical leave)

 - After 2 years in the company you get 5 extra vacation days, and 5 more after 4 years

 - Extra free day for your birthday 🥳 (you can take it any day of the month)

 - 4 administrative days per year (non-accumulable)

 - Annual company retreats

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer, iOS & Mac
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

At Copilot Money, our mission is to help the world stress less about money by providing financial clarity for all.



We're a small team building a new kind of financial platform that empowers people to take control of their finances, make informed decisions, and achieve their goals. As a dynamic, early-stage startup, we're looking for passionate individuals to join our team and make a real impact on people's financial lives.



The Position

We're seeking a Senior Product Designer to join our growing team. In this role, you’ll design features primarily for our iOS and Mac apps, reporting to our Head of Design.



This is a hybrid position based in New York City. You’ll be expected to work from our Brooklyn office (in Greenpoint) twice a week along with the rest of the team. While we work from home the rest of the week, the office is always available in case you’d want to go there more often.



What you’ll do

 - Prototype, design, and iterate on features to turn complex user problems into intuitive solutions for our native iPhone, iPad, and Mac apps.

 - Collaborate with designers, engineers, and customer success to refine your designs based on user feedback, data, and technical constraints.

 - Conduct user research and testing in partnership with customer success.

 - Shape the direction of our apps, and contribute to the design system to maintain consistency across platforms, and accelerate engineering handoff.



Who you are

 - 6+ years of product design experience, with a focus on mobile and consumer apps

 - Deep knowledge of Figma (or Sketch) and the Apple Human Interface Guidelines

 - Exceptional visual and interaction design skills, with a strong eye for detail

 - Autonomous, you work independently and move forward your own projects

 - Strong communication skills, ability to articulate design decisions to a wide audience

 - Based in New York City



Why Copilot Money?

 - Be part of a mission-driven company that's making a real difference in people's financial lives

 - Work on challenging problems at the intersection of technology and personal finance

 - Join an inclusive culture with curious and compassionate colleagues

 - Enjoy the benefits of a small team: close interaction with teammates, opportunities to wear many hats, and the ability to see your impact directly

 - Collaborate in a fast-moving, iterative environment with a close connection to our passionate user base.



Compensation and Benefits

 - The expected salary range for this role is $175,000 - $205,000. The starting base pay will depend on factors including skills, experience, market, and internal pay parity.

 - Competitive equity in the company

 - Health, Dental & Vision Insurance (company pays 100% premium for employee)

 - 401k matching up to 4%

 - Home office setup, and monthly Internet reimbursement (up to $80/month)

 - Wellness Reimbursement ($100/month)

 - 4 weeks of PTO. After 2 years in the company you get 5 extra vacation days, and 5 more after 4 years.

 - Annual company retreats.



At Copilot Money, we believe that diverse teams build better products. We strongly encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.



Join us in our mission to help the world stress less about money and create financial clarity for all!

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

We're a small team that believes providing access to better financial insights and tools improves people’s lives, so we’re taking money matters into our own hands.



Our product https://copilot.money is a personal finance management app for iOS and Mac that people actually love using https://twitter.com/copilotmoney. Copilot takes advantage of machine learning to deliver powerful, hyper-personalized finance data insights to help users effortlessly understand and manage their spending, budgeting, and investing. We also use crowdsourced feedback to continuously improve the experience.



The Position

We’ve built a best-in-class app for iOS and Mac, and now we’re expanding to Web and Android with the same high-quality user experience. To support this, we need robust backend APIs and infrastructure that can scale efficiently across platforms.



We’re looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to play a key role in designing and developing these backend systems. You’ll collaborate closely with Product and Design teams to scope, build, and optimize APIs and services that power Copilot across all platforms.



This is a hybrid position based in Santiago, Chile, you’ll be required to come into our office at least 2 times a week along with the rest of the team. We work from home the rest of the week, but the office is always available in case you want to make use of it more often.



Your Responsibilities

 - Design, build, and maintain backend services and APIs that support iOS, macOS, Android, and Web applications.

 - Contribute to architecture and infrastructure discussions, recommending improvements to ensure scalability, security, and performance.

 - Collaborate with Product and Client teams to translate requirements into technical solutions.

 - Participate in code reviews, ensuring best practices and high-quality standards.

 - Contribute to ongoing improvements in developer experience and system reliability.

 - Support the team in technical decision-making and mentor junior engineers as needed.
   
   

Required Qualifications

 - 5+ years of experience working on backend services.

 - Strong proficiency in Node.js / JavaScript (TypeScript a plus)

 - Experience with Google Cloud Platform

 - Hands-on experience with API design and architecture for mobile and web applications.

 - English proficiency — all meetings and internal communications are conducted in English.

 - Team player who can manage time independently.

 - Based in Santiago, Chile



Desired Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or equivalent.

 - Experience with Firebase / Firestore, CI/CD systems, and monitoring tools.

 - Familiarity with performance optimization, scalability, and alerting infrastructure.

 - Prior exposure to mentoring or supporting peers in a collaborative engineering environment.



Compensation and Benefits

 - Monthly salary of CLP $3.000.000 - $3.600.000 (líquido).

 - Equity in the company (Stock options).

 - Home Office Setup.

 - Complementary health insurance (in process).

 - Remote work allowance (CLP $50.000/month).

 - Wellness allowance (CLP $85.000/month).

 - We cover sick days that your health insurance doesn’t pay (usually the first 3 days of a medical leave).

 - After 2 years in the company you get 5 extra vacation days, and 5 more after 4 years.

 - Extra free day for your birthday 🥳 (you can take it any day of the month).

 - 4 administrative days per year (non-accumulable).

 - Annual company retreats.



Why should you work with us?

A product people love: Copilot users are extremely engaged with the app and are sharing it with their friends and family.

A team that cares: We’re a bunch of geeks who care about data quality and good coffee. We go the extra mile when it comes to making our users happy (and making an excellent pour-over).

Significant impact: We are currently a small, twenty-five-person team with big ambitions. Our team has previous experience at companies like Google and

source_jobs.title: Senior iOS Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

We're a small team that believes providing access to better financial insights and tools improves people’s lives, so we’re taking money matters into our own hands.

Our product is a personal finance management app for iOS and Mac that people actually love using. Copilot takes advantage of machine learning to deliver powerful, hyper-personalized finance data insights to help users effortlessly understand and manage their spending, budgeting, and investing. We also use crowdsourced feedback to continuously improve the experience.



The Position

In this role, you’ll be part of our Apple Platforms team, developing our native iOS, macOS (Catalyst-based) and iPad apps. You'll work closely with our Product, Design, and Backend teams to define and build new features. You’ll also help us maintain and optimize our current features.



This is a hybrid position based in New York City, you’ll be required to come into our office in Brooklyn at least 2 times a week along with the rest of the team. We work from home the rest of the week, but the office is always available in case you want to make use of it more often.



Your Responsibilities

 - Collaborate closely with the Product and Design teams to define and implement new features and constantly improve the ones we already have.

 - Maintain and improve the quality and performance of the Apple platform apps.

 - Implement unit and UI tests for new features to maintain and improve reliability.
   
   

Required Qualifications

 - 5+ years of experience developing natively for iOS/Apple platforms.

 - Solid understanding of the app and view lifecycles for UIKit-based apps

 - Experience with UIKit and the Swift programming language.

 - An eye for good design, and attention to detail.

 - Team player who can manage time independently.

 - Based in NYC.



Desired Qualifications

 - Bachelor in Computer Science or equivalent.

 - Experience with: Firebase / Firestore, CI / CD systems, and Logging systems.



Compensation and Benefits

 - Annual Salary of $175k to $205k

 - Equity in the company

 - Health, Dental & Vision Insurance (company pays 100% premium for employee)

 - 401k matching up to 4%

 - Home Office Setup, and monthly Internet reimbursement (up to $80/month)

 - Wellness Reimbursement ($100/month)

 - 4 weeks of PTO. After 2 years in the company you get 5 extra vacation days, and 5 more after 4 years.

 - Annual company retreats
   
   

Why should you work with us?

A product people love: Copilot users are extremely engaged with the app and are sharing it with their friends and family.

A team that cares: We’re a bunch of geeks who care about data quality and good coffee. We go the extra mile when it comes to making our users happy (and making an excellent pour-over).

Significant impact: We are currently a small, twenty-five-person team with big ambitions. Our team has previous experience at companies like Google and Apple.

Multicultural team: We’re based in NYC 🇺🇸 and in Santiago, Chile 🇨🇱.

Great company culture: You’ll work with a highly collaborative team that’s always learning and ready to help each other out.



At Copilot Money, we believe that diverse teams build better products. We strongly encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

Join us in our mission to help the world stress less about money and create financial clarity for all!

notes: High-energy, fast-paced startup with a strong engineering core.
